danger_signals.visual_form: Defensive player spacing and movement patterns
Yardage counter moving backward or stalling

safety_signals.meaning: Viable passing target with low interception risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Blocker positioning between quarterback and defense
Yardage counter advancing toward opponent endzone

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent team uses contrasting color palette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Home field or neutral stadium designation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Defensive player uniform distinct from player team
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Jersey color and number assignment differs from player unit
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Away field or neutral stadium context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Offensive player formation displayed on field grid; ball in quarterback or running back control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player team executes predetermined play calls; defense reacts to formation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Defensive formation orientation; offensive players in transitional state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defense positions to intercept or tackle; offense adjusts route assignments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ball possession indicator flips to opposing team; field position may reset
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player control switches to opposing team; formations realign

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score counter increments for active team; visual celebration or score animation triggers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Play pauses; ball returns to starting position for new drive

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Yardage marker moves based on penalty type; penalty indicator displays
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Field position adjusts; replay initiates with new starting point

environmental_storytelling.example: Real-time score, down marker, and yardage information communicate game momentum and remaining opportunities
Yard-line proximity to endzone indicates scoring potential and drive urgency
Clock display in two-minute drill mode signals urgency and play-call constraints
Team roster display allows player identification and formation strategy customization

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Positioning at line of scrimmage; awaiting play execution
Drops back into pocket; scans receiver options; releases or scrambles based on coverage
Hands off to running back or executes keeper; evades defenders
Navigates field grid toward endzone; avoids defensive tackle zones; cuts based on blocking
Follows assigned route pattern; adjusts for quarterback scramble; becomes vulnerable to coverage
Maintains zone or man coverage; reacts to ball trajectory; pursues ball carrier or intercepts
Blocks designated defenders; collapses if overpowered; allows quarterback pocket time

notes: Joe Montana Football is an arcade-style sports simulation with simplified rule set (13 plays vs. 113 in competitor Madden). Game communicates world-state through standard football UI conventions: scoreboard, field grid, team color differentiation, and formation visualization. No narrative elements, factions, danger/safety signals operate within standard gridiron mechanics (coverage vs. route separation, field position advancement, scoring). Visual communication is purely tactical and functional rather than narrative or world-building focused. Game features 16 generic teams without official NFL licensing, reducing faction differentiation beyond color and uniform design.
